variant: compare 2 acne treatment options — include timeframes like 'week 4 — visible change' in the details

request.message: compare 2 acne treatment options — include timeframes like 'week 4 — visible change' in the details

response_body.response:
**best move:** stack them. azelaic AM + tretinoin PM is the standard acne protocol.

start azelaic 2–3×/week first to stabilize your barrier, then introduce tretinoin at 0.05% once skin tolerates it. you'll see redness drop in week 1–2 from azelaic, then real texture change by week 4–6 when tretinoin kicks in.

products.name: The Ordinary Azelaic Acid Suspension 10%
products.description: Azelaic acid for redness, PIH, and mild rosacea — pregnancy-safe.

products.name: Beauty of Joseon Relief Sun Rice + Probiotic SPF 50+ PA++++
products.description: Cosmetic-elegant Asian SPF — chemical filters, no white cast.

options.name: azelaic acid (AM)
pros: week 1–2 — reduces redness and bacterial load
cons: slower than prescription retinoids for severe acne
may irritate if barrier already damaged

options.name: tretinoin 0.05% (PM)
pros: week 4–6, visible cell turnover and clearing
week 8+, significant texture and pigment improvement
gold standard for acne and anti-aging
pea-sized on dry skin, gradually increase strength
cons: requires barrier stability first (risk of irritation)
cannot layer with other actives same night
